live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards args.owner into the service call
Orchestrates owner resolution, base-record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission
Derives effective_owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or config/defaults.json
Builds the stored/base record with name, status, and resolved owner only
Builds routing_key from the resolved owner and item name after owner resolution
Emits the final payload by copying the base record and attaching owner_source and routing_key

owner_source.logic: Returns explicit when a non-empty owner argument is provided; otherwise returns default from config/defaults.json.

routing_key.logic: Slugifies owner and name, then formats owner_slug:name_slug.

emission.note: owner_source and routing_key are appended during serialization, after the base record returns from storage.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner path emits owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
Missing --owner path emits owner_source=default and routing_key derived from the configured default owner.
CLI passes --owner through to the service path without dropping owner_source or routing_key.
Observed runtime output matched the explicit-owner test payload.
Observed runtime output matched the default-owner test payload; the routing_key string stays the same here because config/defaults.json also sets owner to pm-oncall.

rejected_decoys.reason: Unused legacy helper; not referenced by the live sync path.
Unused legacy helper; not referenced by the live sync path.
Unused draft helper; no live caller derives owner_source from storage through this function.

The note claims owner_source looks storage-derived and routing_key is computed before CLI owner application; both claims are contradicted by sync_app/service.py::sync_item and sync_app/serializer.py::serialize_payload.
